1.Title: Severe Response Time Degradation
1.Insight: The endpoint Homepage experienced a **dramatic increase** in response time, reaching nearly **40,000 ms** under steady load, indicating probable server-side performance bottlenecks or resource exhaustion.
1.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) increased from 22,300 ms at 9:40 AM UTC to a peak of **39,880 ms** at 9:41 AM UTC for Homepage. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained stable at 10 until 9:41 AM UTC, while [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) dropped to 0.4 RPS at 9:41 AM UTC and 9:41 AM UTC, then fluctuated between 0.4 and 0.6 RPS from 9:41 AM UTC to 9:41 AM UTC.
1.Recommendation: Consider investigating server-side scalability and resource constraints such as thread pool exhaustion, database connection limits, or unoptimized code paths. Increase server capacity or optimize endpoints to handle sustained load.

2.Title: Drop in Request Throughput
2.Insight: Homepage saw a **60% drop** in requests per second despite stable virtual user count, suggesting the server may be throttling or struggling to keep up with demand.
2.Details: [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) remained stable at 1.0 RPS from 9:40 AM UTC to 9:40 AM UTC, then dropped to **0.4 RPS** at 9:41 AM UTC, coinciding with [Response time](#ClientMetrics) peaking at 39,880 ms. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) stayed at 10 until 9:41 AM UTC, indicating clients were still attempting requests but receiving delayed responses.
2.Recommendation: Investigate potential server-side throttling mechanisms, queue bottlenecks, or response delays. Review slow log diagnostics and consider load balancing or horizontal scaling.

3.Title: Virtual User Attrition Under Load
3.Insight: Virtual users for Homepage **decreased by 50%** as response times peaked, implying client test infrastructure was forced to reduce load—likely due to timeouts or prolonged wait times.
3.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) dropped from 10 at 9:41 AM UTC to **5** at 9:41 AM UTC, with [Response time](#ClientMetrics) stabilizing around 35,100 ms after these drops. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) also remained low, indicating load generator could not sustain higher concurrency.
3.Recommendation: Review client-side timeout settings and consider optimizing server performance to support higher concurrency. If possible, distribute load generation across additional engines to minimize single-point bottlenecks.

4.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed with performance degradation. Response times increased steadily to a peak of nearly 40s and throughput dropped, indicating slow responses likely due to backend constraints.
